<<Economic Stress Analysis>>
The maximum substance abuse incidents occur at an economic stress index of 2, yielding 13 incidents per 1000 people.
To keep incidents below 20, the community must maintain an economic stress index between 0 and approximately 3.37.
This data helps human planners understand the curve like a baseball trajectory peaking and falling.
Western nations often study such water-related stressors alongside economic factors to improve public health.
Maintaining the index within this range ensures safety for all residents.
